name: Buttermilk Lemon Pound Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and flour a 10-inch bundt pan or two 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2 inch loaf pans.
2. In a bowl, gently warm the uncracked eggs by pouring hot tap water over them. Let stand for several minutes to warm the eggs.
3. Sift together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt onto a large piece of waxed paper.
4. Add the butter to a large mixing bowl and beat until smooth and creamy.
5. Gradually add the sugar, beating constantly, and continue beating until smooth and well blended.
6. Add all the eggs at once and beat until the mixture is light and fluffy.
7. Mix about 1/2 of the flour mixture into the butter mixture.
8. Stir in the lemon extract and lemon rind into the buttermilk.
9. Beat in half the buttermilk mixture into the batter.
10. Add the remaining flour and buttermilk mixtures, and beat until the batter is smooth and well blended.
11. Pour the batter into the prepared pan(s).
12. Bake the bundt cake at 350°F for 1 to 1 1/4 hours, or until a pick inserted in the center of a cake comes out clean. For loaf cakes, bake for 40 to 45 minutes.
13. Remove from the oven and let cool on a rack for 5 minutes before turning out onto the rack to cool completely before serving.
